subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of marriage rites of their culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Identify the processes involved in a traditional marriage.
examplars:
1. Discuss the traditional marriage process in their community. • E.g. investigation (knocking), presentation of items, the marriage ceremony, and so on.
2. List the items used in traditional marriage in your ethnic community.
3. Discuss the significance of the items and the processes in traditional marriage.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS — OCCUPATIONAL, CIRCUMSTANTIAL, STOOL AND RELIGIOUS NAMES
content_standard: B9.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of other naming systems in the ethnic community
indicator_description: Identify and explain circumstantial and occupational names in their community.
examplars:
1. Identify circumstantial names (e.g. names related to time, place, or events of birth) and occupational names.
2. Discuss the reasons why such names are given to children.
3. Compare these names with other Ghanaian cultures.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: CHIEFTAINCY — THE ROLE OF THE MODERN CHIEF
content_standard: B9.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the role of the modern chief in community and national development
indicator_description: Discuss the changing roles of chiefs in modern Ghanaian society.
examplars:
1. Identify the traditional roles of a chief.
2. Discuss how these roles have evolved to include modern development needs.
3. Examine the relationship between chiefs and the central government.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate use of appropriate language in various discourse contexts
indicator_description: Use appropriate language for interviews and formal interactions.
examplars:
1. Identify the language features for conducting interviews.
2. Prepare questions for an interview with a prominent member of the community.
3. Role-play an interview session using appropriate registers.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the phonological rules of the language
indicator_description: Explain and identify the process of vowel harmony in the language.
examplars:
1. Divide vowels into harmonic sets (e.g. Set 1 and Set 2).
2. Identify how vowels in a word agree based on harmonic features.
3. Provide examples of words that demonstrate vowel harmony rules.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to read and evaluate various literary and non-literary texts
indicator_description: Evaluate the writer's style and use of literary devices in a text.
examplars:
1. Read a selected text and identify literary devices such as metaphors, similes, and personification.
2. Discuss the effect of these devices on the meaning of the text.
3. Critique the structure and coherence of the text.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.3.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to translate complex texts between English and the Ghanaian Language
indicator_description: Translate idiomatic expressions and complex paragraphs accurately.
examplars:
1. Identify idioms in a text and find their equivalents in the target language.
2. Translate a passage while preserving the stylistic nuances of the original.
3. Compare translations and discuss the best choices for clarity and impact.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (CLAUSES AND PHRASES)
content_standard: B9.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the structure and function of clauses and phrases
indicator_description: Identify and use different types of clauses and phrases in writing.
examplars:
1. Identify noun, adjectival, and adverbial phrases in sentences.
2. Use dependent and independent clauses to build complex sentences.
3. Analyze the function of specific clauses within a paragraph.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.5.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to write creative and imaginative compositions
indicator_description: Write a creative piece (e.g. story, poem, or play) using appropriate conventions.
examplars:
1. Brainstorm ideas for an original creative work.
2. Draft the creative piece ensuring the use of rich vocabulary and literary devices.
3. Peer-review and refine the work for publication or presentation.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B9.6.1.1 Respond to and appreciate various genres of literature critically
indicator_description: Provide a critical analysis of a selected literary work.
examplars:
1. Discuss the major themes and their relevance to contemporary society.
2. Analyze character development and plot structure.
3. Write a critical essay on the strengths and weaknesses of the literary work.
